content.text:
Sharp — black/white base with yellow accent, plus dark/light theme support via Tailwind. Let me lock this in:

**Color system updated:**
- **Dark theme**: `#000000` / `#0a0a0a` backgrounds, white text, `#262626` borders
- **Light theme**: `#ffffff` / `#fafafa` backgrounds, black text, `#e5e5e5` borders
- **Accent**: Tailwind amber/yellow — `amber-400` (`#fbbf24`) primary, `amber-500` (`#f59e0b`) hover
- **Theme toggle**: Sun/moon icon in nav, respects `prefers-color-scheme` as default
- **The Raven logo**: Works on both themes (black bird on light, white bird on dark)

Yellow accent used sparingly for: CTA buttons, active tab indicators, highlighted badges, the chat widget send button, pricing "recommended" badge.

Now section flow was approved. Let me present the **problem statement section** — this is the emotional hook right below the hero.

**Section 3: Problem Statement**

> **"Your team's knowledge is scattered. Your people are stuck."**

Three pain-point cards:

| Card | Headline | Copy |
|------|----------|------|
| 1 | **Docs nobody reads** | Knowledge lives in PDFs, wikis, and Confluence pages that go stale. Your team asks the same questions on Slack instead. |
| 2 | **Search that doesn't work** | Keyword search returns 50 results. None of them answer the actual question. Your team gives up and pings someone senior. |
| 3 | **No way to just ask** | Your team wants to ask a question and get an answer — with a source. Not browse. Not search. Just ask. |

Then a transition line:
> **"Raven fixes this. Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp."**

Does this problem statement content land right, or want to adjust the tone/messaging?

content.text:
**Section 5: How It Works — 3-step flow**

| Step | Headline | Copy |
|------|----------|------|
| 1 | **Ingest** | Upload PDFs, DOCX, Markdown, HTML, or paste URLs. Raven chunks, embeds, and indexes everything automatically. |
| 2 | **Search** | Hybrid retrieval — pgvector cosine similarity + BM25 full-text ranking fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. No keyword guessing. |
| 3 | **Answer** | Your team asks in natural language. Raven responds with accurate, cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. |

Minimal visual — three numbered cards in a horizontal row with an arrow/line connecting them. Technical details surface on hover or in small muted text below each card.

**Section 6: Deploy Your Way — 4 options**

| Option | Icon | Copy |
|--------|------|------|
| Docker Compose | 🐳 | One command. Full stack. Perfect for getting started and small teams. |
| Kubernetes | ☸️ | Production-grade, multi-region, auto-scaling. Helm charts included. |
| EC2 + Ansible | 🖥️ | Cloud VM with auto-TLS via Traefik. Playbooks ready to go. |
| Edge / Raspberry Pi | 📡 | 25MB ARM64 binary. Run the API on a Pi, connect a remote AI worker via gRPC. |

**Section 7: Enterprise Ready — feature cards**

| Card | Copy |
|------|------|
| **Multi-Tenant Isolation** | Full Row-Level Security per tenant in PostgreSQL. Your data never crosses boundaries. |
| **SSO / SCIM** | Keycloak-powered OIDC, SAML, and SCIM provisioning out of the box. |
| **Audit Logs** | Immutable, timestamped audit trail for every action. Compliance-ready. |
| **BYOK** | Bring your own LLM keys — Anthropic, OpenAI, Cohere, or self-hosted models. Zero vendor lock-in. |
| **eBPF WAF** | Kernel-level XDP pre-filtering and observability at the edge. |
| **GDPR / SOC2** | Built for compliance. Self-host for full data sovereignty. |

Do sections 5, 6, and 7 look right?

input.content:
# Raven Landing Page — Design Spec

**Date**: 2026-04-13
**Domain**: raven.ravencloak.org
**Status**: Approved

---

## Overview

A full-platform marketing landing page for Raven — an open-source, self-hostable, multi-tenant AI knowledge platform with chat, voice, and WhatsApp capabilities. The page follows a demo-led approach with an embedded live `<raven-chat>` widget in the hero section.

## Target Audience

- **Primary**: Developers and technical leads evaluating Raven for their team
- **Secondary**: Business decision makers (CTO/VP Eng) looking for enterprise features and compliance
- **Approach**: Developer-first copy with enterprise trust signals layered in

## Design System

### Color Palette (Tailwind)

| Token | Dark Theme | Light Theme | Usage |
|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| Background primary | `#000000` / `black` | `#ffffff` / `white` | Page background |
| Background secondary | `#0a0a0a` / `neutral-950` | `#fafafa` / `neutral-50` | Card/section backgrounds |
| Background tertiary | `#1a1a1a` / `neutral-900` | `#f5f5f5` / `neutral-100` | Inset panels, code blocks |
| Border | `#262626` / `neutral-800` | `#e5e5e5` / `neutral-200` | Card borders, dividers |
| Border hover | `#404040` / `neutral-700` | `#d4d4d4` / `neutral-300` | Interactive borders |
| Text primary | `#ffffff` / `white` | `#000000` / `black` | Headlines, body |
| Text secondary | `#a3a3a3` / `neutral-400` | `#525252` / `neutral-600` | Descriptions, subtext |
| Text muted | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | Captions, labels |
| Accent | `#fbbf24` / `amber-400` | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | CTAs, active indicators, highlights |
| Accent hover | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | `#d97706` / `amber-600` | Button hover states |

### Theme Behavior

- Dark/light toggle in nav (sun/moon icon)
- Default: respects `prefers-color-scheme` system preference
- Implemented via Tailwind `dark:` class variant on `<html>` element
- Raven logo: black bird on light theme, white bird on dark theme

### Typography

- **Headlines**: Bold/extrabold, larger sizes
- **Section headers**: Bold, provocative tone (problem-first hooks)
- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible language
- **Technical details**: Muted text, smaller size, surfaces in feature deep-dives

### Content Tone

- **Section headers**: Bold and opinionated — *"Your team's knowledge is trapped. Free it."*
- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible — *"Ingest any document, ask in natural language, get cited answers."*
- **Feature deep-dives**: Technical specifics — *"Hybrid search combining pgvector cosine similarity with BM25 full-text ranking"*
- **GitHub/README**: Engineering-precise tone (separate from landing page)

## Page Structure

### Section 1: Navigation Bar

**Layout**: Sticky top nav, full-width, blurred background

**Elements**:
- Left: Raven logo (themed — black on light, white on dark)
- Center: Features, Pricing, Docs, GitHub (with stars badge)
- Right: Theme toggle (sun/moon), Sign In (ghost button), Get Started Free (amber accent button)

**Mobile**: Hamburger menu, Get Started Free always visible

### Section 2: Hero (Centered, Demo-Led)

**Layout**: Centered text block → trust badges → live chat widget

**Content**:
- Badge: `Open Source · MIT Licensed` (small uppercase, amber text)
- Headline: **"The AI Brain for Your Entire Team"**
- Subhead: "Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. Self-host or use our cloud."
- CTAs: `Get Started Free` (amber filled) + `Try Live Demo ↓` (ghost/outline)
- Trust bar: 🔓 MIT Licensed · 🐳 4 Deploy Options · 🔑 Bring Your Own LLM Keys · 🛡️ Full Data Sovereignty

**Live Demo Widget**:
- Embedded `<raven-chat>` web component
- Connected to a demo knowledge base (Raven's own docs)
- Header: "💬 Try Raven Live — Ask anything" + "Demo Knowledge Base" badge
- Pre-populated with a sample Q&A showing cited answer
- Input field with Send button (amber accent)

### Section 3: Problem Statement

**Headline**: "Your team's knowledge is scattered. Your people are stuck."

**Layout**: 3 cards in a row (stacks on mobile)

| Card | Headline | Copy |
|------|----------|------|
| 1 | Docs nobody reads | Knowledge lives in PDFs, wikis, and Confluence pages that go stale. Your team asks the same questions on Slack instead. |
| 2 | Search that doesn't work | Keyword search returns 50 results. None of them answer the actual question. Your team gives up and pings someone senior. |
| 3 | No way to just ask | Your team wants to ask a question and get an answer — with a source. Not browse. Not search. Just ask. |

**Transition line**: "Raven fixes this. Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp."

### Section 4: Tabbed Features

**Layout**: 3 tabs with active indicator (amber underline). Each tab shows screenshot on one side + description on the other. Alternating layout (screenshot left/right) for visual rhythm.

**Tab 1: AI Chat**
- Headline: "Ask your docs anything"
- Copy: Semantic search powered by hybrid vector + BM25 ranking. Every answer comes with source citations. Embed the chatbot widget on your site with a single `<raven-chat>` tag.
- Visual: Screenshot of chat interface with cited answer

**Tab 2: Voice Agent**
- Headline: "Talk to your knowledge base"
- Copy: Real-time voice calls via WebRTC. Hands-free access to your entire knowledge base — powered by LiveKit with STT, LLM, and TTS pipeline. Perfect for field teams and mobile workers.
- Visual: Screenshot of voice call UI

**Tab 3: WhatsApp**
- Headline: "Meet your team where they already are"
- Copy: Connect via WhatsApp Business API. Zero app installs, zero training. Your team asks questions in the chat they already use every day.
- Visual: Screenshot of WhatsApp conversation with Raven

### Section 5: How It Works

**Layout**: 3 numbered cards in horizontal row, connected by a line/arrow

| Step | Headline | Copy |
|------|----------|------|
| 1 | **Ingest** | Upload PDFs, DOCX, Markdown, HTML, or paste URLs. Raven chunks, embeds, and indexes everything automatically. |
| 2 | **Search** | Hybrid retrieval — pgvector cosine similarity + BM25 full-text ranking fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. No keyword guessing. |
| 3 | **Answer** | Your team asks in natural language. Raven responds with accurate, cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. |

Technical details in muted text below each card for developer audience.

### Section 6: Deploy Your Way

**Headline**: "Your infrastructure. Your rules."

**Layout**: 4 cards in a grid (2x2 on desktop, stacks on mobile)

| Option | Icon | Copy |
|--------|------|------|
| Docker Compose | 🐳 | One command. Full stack. Perfect for getting started and small teams. |
| Kubernetes | ☸️ | Production-grade, multi-region, auto-scaling. Helm charts included. |
| EC2 + Ansible | 🖥️ | Cloud VM with auto-TLS via Traefik. Playbooks ready to go. |
| Edge / Raspberry Pi | 📡 | 25MB ARM64 binary. Run the API on a Pi, connect a remote AI worker via gRPC. |

### Section 7: Enterprise Ready

**Headline**: "Built for teams that don't compromise."

**Layout**: 6 cards in a 3x2 grid (stacks on mobile)

| Card | Copy |
|------|------|
| Multi-Tenant Isolation | Full Row-Level Security per tenant in PostgreSQL. Your data never crosses boundaries. |
| SSO / SCIM | Keycloak-powered OIDC, SAML, and SCIM provisioning out of the box. |
| Audit Logs | Immutable, timestamped audit trail for every action. Compliance-ready. |
| BYOK | Bring your own LLM keys — Anthropic, OpenAI, Cohere, or self-hosted models. Zero vendor lock-in. |
| eBPF WAF | Kernel-level XDP pre-filtering and observability at the edge. |
| GDPR / SOC2 | Built for compliance. Self-host for full data sovereignty. |

### Section 8: Pricing

**Headline**: "Simple pricing. Generous free tier."

**Layout**: 3 tier cards side-by-side. Pro card has amber "Recommended" badge and slightly elevated/bordered treatment.

| | Free | Pro (Recommended) | Enterprise |
|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $0 forever | $29/month | Contact Sales |
| Users | 5 | 25 | Unlimited |
| Workspaces | 2 | 10 | Unlimited |
| Knowledge Bases | 3 | 50 | Unlimited |
| Storage | 500 MB | 10 GB | Unlimited |
| Voice Sessions | 1 concurrent | 5 concurrent | Unlimited |
| Voice Minutes | 60/mo | 1,200/mo | Unlimited |
| Key Features | Full MIT source, AI chat/voice/WhatsApp, BYOK, self-host | Everything in Free + higher limits, advanced rate limiting | Everything in Pro + SAML/SSO/SCIM, audit logs, eBPF WAF, dedicated support, custom SLA |
| CTA | Get Started Free | Start Pro Trial | Contact Sales |

### Section 9: Bottom CTA

**Headline**: "Start building with Raven in under 5 minutes."

**Code snippet**: `docker compose up -d`

**CTA**: `Get Started Free` (amber accent, large)

### Section 10: Footer

**Layout**: 4-column grid (stacks on mobile)

| Product | Resources | Community | Legal |
|---------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Features | Quick Start | GitHub | Privacy Policy |
| Pricing | API Reference | Discord | Terms of Service |
| Docs | Architecture | Contributing | MIT License |
| Changelog | Blog | Roadmap | |

**Bottom row**: © 2026 Ravencloak · Built with ❤️ for teams that own their data

## Technical Implementation

### Stack
- **Framework**: Static HTML + Tailwind CSS (paid license — Tailwind Plus/UI components)
- **Theme**: Tailwind `dark:` class variant, toggled via JavaScript on `<html>` element
- **Chat Widget**: Embedded `<raven-chat>` web component pointing to demo API
- **Hosting**: Static site at raven.ravencloak.org (Cloudflare Pages or similar CDN)
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first, breakpoints at `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`

### Assets Required
- Raven logo (SVG, themed — black and white variants)
- Product screenshots for Chat, Voice, WhatsApp features (from existing admin dashboard)
- Icons for deployment options and enterprise features (Tailwind Heroicons or similar)

### Demo Knowledge Base
- A curated knowledge base containing Raven's own documentation
- Hosted on the production API or a dedicated demo instance
- The `<raven-chat>` widget connects via a domain-scoped API key
- Rate-limited for public access (anonymous users, read-only)

### Performance Targets
- First Contentful Paint < 1.5s
- Total page weight < 500KB (excluding chat widget)
- Lighthouse score > 90 across all categories

## Open Questions

1. **Raven logo**: Does a finalized SVG logo exist, or do we need to create one?
2. **Demo instance**: Is there a running demo API we can point the chat widget to, or do we need to set one up?
3. **Product screenshots**: Can we capture these from the existing admin dashboard, or do we need to mock them?
4. **Domain setup**: Is raven.ravencloak.org DNS already configured?

## Out of Scope

- User authentication / sign-up flow (handled by app.ravencloak.org)
- Blog / changelog pages (future addition)
- Documentation site (separate docs.ravencloak.org)
- A/B testing / analytics integration (PostHog — future addition)

## Overview

A full-platform marketing landing page for Raven — an open-source, self-hostable, multi-tenant AI knowledge platform with chat, voice, and WhatsApp capabilities. The page follows a demo-led approach with an embedded live `<raven-chat>` widget in the hero section.

## Target Audience

- **Primary**: Developers and technical leads evaluating Raven for their team
- **Secondary**: Business decision makers (CTO/VP Eng) looking for enterprise features and compliance
- **Approach**: Developer-first copy with enterprise trust signals layered in

## Design System

### Color Palette (Tailwind)

| Token | Dark Theme | Light Theme | Usage |
|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| Background primary | `#000000` / `black` | `#ffffff` / `white` | Page background |
| Background secondary | `#0a0a0a` / `neutral-950` | `#fafafa` / `neutral-50` | Card/section backgrounds |
| Background tertiary | `#1a1a1a` / `neutral-900` | `#f5f5f5` / `neutral-100` | Inset panels, code blocks |
| Border | `#262626` / `neutral-800` | `#e5e5e5` / `neutral-200` | Card borders, dividers |
| Border hover | `#404040` / `neutral-700` | `#d4d4d4` / `neutral-300` | Interactive borders |
| Text primary | `#ffffff` / `white` | `#000000` / `black` | Headlines, body |
| Text secondary | `#a3a3a3` / `neutral-400` | `#525252` / `neutral-600` | Descriptions, subtext |
| Text muted | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | Captions, labels |
| Accent | `#fbbf24` / `amber-400` | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | CTAs, active indicators, highlights |
| Accent hover | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | `#d97706` / `amber-600` | Button hover states |

### Theme Behavior

- Dark/light toggle in nav (sun/moon icon)
- Default: respects `prefers-color-scheme` system preference
- Implemented via Tailwind `dark:` class variant on `<html>` element
- Raven logo: black bird on light theme, white bird on dark theme

### Typography

- **Headlines**: Bold/extrabold, larger sizes
- **Section headers**: Bold, provocative tone (problem-first hooks)
- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible language
- **Technical details**: Muted text, smaller size, surfaces in feature deep-dives

### Content Tone

- **Section headers**: Bold and opinionated — *"Your team's knowledge is trapped. Free it."*
- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible — *"Ingest any document, ask in natural language, get cited answers."*
- **Feature deep-dives**: Technical specifics — *"Hybrid search combining pgvector cosine similarity with BM25 full-text ranking"*
- **GitHub/README**: Engineering-precise tone (separate from landing page)

**Mobile**: Hamburger menu, Get Started Free always visible

---

## Design Spec Review: Raven Landing Page

**File**: `/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-13-raven-landing-page-design.md`

---

### What the spec does well

- The section structure is logical and follows a proven SaaS landing page funnel: problem, solution, features, social proof, pricing, CTA.
- The design token table is unusually thorough for a spec -- it provides both hex values and Tailwind class names for both themes, which will make implementation fast and unambiguous.
- The pricing table matches the backend model exactly. I verified the tiers, limits, and prices against `internal/model/billing.go` (`DefaultPlans()`), and the numbers are a 1:1 match: Free (5 users, 2 workspaces, 3 KBs, 500 MB, 1 concurrent voice, 60 min/mo), Pro ($29/mo, 25 users, etc.), Enterprise ($99/mo backend but "Contact Sales" on the landing page -- see issue below).
- The `<raven-chat>` web component is real and production-ready. It exists at `frontend/src/components/chat-widget/`, supports all the attributes the spec implies (api-key, api-url, theme-color, voice-enabled, etc.), and has a clean custom-element registration path.

---

### MUST-FIX Issues

**1. Enterprise pricing discrepancy between spec and backend**

The spec says Enterprise is "Contact Sales" with no listed price. However, `internal/model/billing.go` defines the enterprise plan at `PriceMonthly: 9900` ($99.00/mo). The spec needs to decide: is Enterprise truly custom/contact-sales, or is it a self-serve $99/mo tier? If "Contact Sales" is the intended landing page presentation, that is fine for marketing, but the spec should explicitly note the discrepancy so implementers do not accidentally show "$99/mo" somewhere. Add a one-line note like "Enterprise is priced at $99/mo in the backend but presented as Contact Sales on the landing page."

**2. Color scheme completely contradicts the existing landing page**

The spec mandates a black/white + amber accent palette. The existing `landing/index.html` (already shipped) uses an indigo/purple (`raven-500: #6366f1`) color scheme with a purple gradient hero, purple buttons, and no amber anywhere. The chat widget's default theme color is also `#6366f1` (indigo), not amber. The spec needs to acknowledge this is a full redesign, not an iteration. It should state whether the existing `landing/index.html` is being replaced wholesale or whether this is a new page at a different path. Without this clarity, an implementer might try to incrementally modify the existing page and create an incoherent hybrid.

**3. Chat widget API is still mock-only -- demo will not work as described**

The spec says the hero will contain a "live" `<raven-chat>` widget "connected to a demo knowledge base" with "pre-populated sample Q&A showing cited answer." I verified `frontend/src/components/chat-widget/chat-api.ts` and it is entirely a mock implementation with `TODO: Replace mock implementation with real SSE fetch` comments. It returns canned random responses, not real RAG answers, and does not produce citations. The spec should either (a) add a prerequisite task to implement the real SSE endpoint before the landing page, or (b) revise the demo description to say "mock-mode demo" and drop the "cited answer" claim, or (c) list the real API endpoint as a dependency in the Open Questions section.

**4. "Helm charts included" claim has no backing artifact**

Section 6 (Deploy Your Way) states Kubernetes deployment with "Helm charts included." There is no `deploy/kubernetes/` directory, no `helm/` directory, and no Helm chart files anywhere in the repository. Either remove "Helm charts included" from the spec copy, or add a task to create them before the landing page ships. Advertising non-existent artifacts on a marketing page is a credibility risk.

**5. "Audit Logs" and "SCIM" enterprise features are not implemented**

Section 7 (Enterprise Ready) claims "Immutable, timestamped audit trail for every action" and "SCIM provisioning out of the box." Searching the codebase, there is no audit log implementation (only references in test stubs and specs) and no SCIM implementation at all. These features exist only in design docs and roadmap. The spec should either label these as "Coming Soon" on the landing page or remove them. Presenting unimplemented features as current capabilities is misleading.

**6. No SEO/meta tags or Open Graph specification**

The existing `landing/index.html` already has `<meta>` tags (og:title, og:description, og:url, twitter:card). The spec defines no meta tags, page title, or OG image. Since this is a full redesign hosted at a different domain (`raven.ravencloak.org` vs the current page's `ravencloak.org`), the spec must include the SEO/meta section to avoid losing existing SEO or shipping without social sharing cards. At minimum: page title, meta description, og:image, og:url, canonical URL, and favicon.

**7. Typography spec lacks concrete values**

The Typography section uses vague descriptors ("Bold/extrabold, larger sizes") without specifying font family, font sizes, line heights, or Tailwind utility classes. For a static HTML + Tailwind implementation, the implementer needs to know: Which font? (The existing page uses Inter.) What are the heading sizes at each breakpoint? Without this, two implementers will produce different-looking pages. At minimum, specify the font family and the size scale (e.g., `text-4xl sm:text-6xl lg:text-7xl` for hero heading).

---

### NICE-TO-HAVE Improvements

**8. Missing font specification**

The existing landing page loads Inter via Google Fonts. The spec should either explicitly carry this forward or specify an alternative. This is downstream of issue 7 but worth a separate callout since font loading has performance implications (the spec targets FCP < 1.5s).

**9. No animation/interaction specification**

The spec describes static content but says nothing about scroll animations, section transitions, hover states beyond color changes, or the tab-switching mechanism for the Features section. The existing page has CSS keyframe animations (`float`, `feature-card:hover`). Should the new page have entrance animations? Smooth scroll? The tabbed features section (Section 4) needs explicit interaction design: how do tabs switch, is there a transition between screenshots, what happens on mobile (do tabs become a vertical stack or a swipeable carousel)?

**10. "Try Live Demo" CTA scroll anchor not defined**

The hero has a `Try Live Demo` ghost button. The spec does not specify what this scrolls to. Presumably it scrolls to the embedded chat widget, but the widget is described as being inside the hero section itself, not below the fold. Clarify whether clicking this button (a) opens the chat widget, (b) smooth-scrolls to a widget below the hero, or (c) navigates to a separate demo page.

**11. GitHub stars badge not specified**

The nav mentions "GitHub (with stars badge)" but does not specify how to fetch the star count (static? GitHub API? shields.io badge?). For a static HTML page, a dynamic star count requires either a client-side fetch to the GitHub API (with rate limiting concerns for anonymous users) or a build-time injection. Specify the approach.

**12. Tailwind CSS distribution strategy unclear**

The spec says "Tailwind CSS (paid license -- Tailwind Plus/UI components)" but the existing landing page uses the CDN play script (`<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com">`). For production at the stated performance targets (<500KB page weight, Lighthouse >90), the CDN play script is inappropriate -- it ships the entire Tailwind runtime (~300KB). The spec should state whether to use Tailwind CLI with purging or Vite + PostCSS. This directly impacts whether the 500KB budget and FCP target are achievable.

**13. Domain inconsistency in existing assets**

The spec targets `raven.ravencloak.org`. The existing `landing/index.html` uses `og:url` of `https://ravencloak.org`. The chat widget's "Powered by" link points to `https://ravenapp.dev`. CTA links point to `app.ravencloak.org`. The spec should provide a definitive domain map (marketing site, app, API, docs) so all links are consistent.

**14. Responsive breakpoint behavior not detailed for key sections**

The spec says "Mobile-first, breakpoints at sm, md, lg, xl" but only gives vague "stacks on mobile" notes for each section. The tabbed features section (Section 4) -- which alternates screenshot left/right -- needs explicit mobile treatment. Do the tabs become an accordion? A vertical list? Does the alternating layout collapse to a consistent order?

**15. No accessibility specification beyond basic semantics**

For a Lighthouse score >90 across all categories (which includes accessibility), the spec should call out: color contrast ratios for the amber accent on black/white backgrounds, focus ring styling, reduced-motion preferences, and aria-label patterns. The amber-400 (#fbbf24) on black passes WCAG AA (contrast ratio ~11.6:1), but amber-400 on the secondary dark background (#0a0a0a) should be verified. More critically, the muted text (#737373) on the dark background (#000000) has a contrast ratio of ~4.6:1 -- it passes AA for normal text but fails AAA.

**16. Open Questions should block implementation**

The Open Questions section lists 4 items (logo SVG, demo API, screenshots, DNS) but marks them as open questions rather than blocking prerequisites. At least items 2 (demo instance) and 4 (DNS) are hard blockers -- you cannot ship a landing page at a domain that is not configured, and the demo-led hero is the centerpiece of the design. These should be upgraded to prerequisites with owners and deadlines.

---

### Summary

The spec is well-structured and the section-by-section content design is strong. The pricing data is verified accurate against the backend. However, there are 7 must-fix issues that would cause implementation confusion or result in a page that makes claims the codebase cannot back up (mock demo presented as live, Helm charts that do not exist, unimplemented enterprise features). The color scheme change from the existing page also needs explicit acknowledgment. Resolving these before writing an implementation plan will prevent rework.

This is a design spec for a full-platform marketing landing page for Raven — an open-source AI knowledge platform. The page will be hosted at raven.ravencloak.org.

Context:
- Raven is a Go+Vue.js+Python platform with chat, voice (LiveKit), and WhatsApp AI capabilities
- Stack: Tailwind CSS (paid license), static HTML, embedded <raven-chat> web component
- Color scheme: Black/white (Raven bird theme) + Tailwind amber accent, dark/light theme support
- Pricing: Free ($0, 5 users) / Pro ($29/mo, 25 users) / Enterprise (custom)
- Payment: Hyperswitch + Razorpay (India-based, no Stripe)
- Demo-led approach: live chatbot widget in the hero section
- Developer-first copy with enterprise trust signals

Review for:
1. Completeness — are any sections missing detail needed for implementation?
2. Consistency — do the design tokens, copy tone, and section structures align throughout?
3. Feasibility — are there any technical concerns with the proposed implementation?
4. Gaps — anything that should be addressed before writing an implementation plan?

Report: list issues as MUST-FIX (blocking) or NICE-TO-HAVE (optional improvements). Keep it concise.

content.content:
1	# Raven Landing Page — Design Spec
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-04-13
4	**Domain**: raven.ravencloak.org
5	**Status**: Approved
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Overview
10	
11	A full-platform marketing landing page for Raven — an open-source, self-hostable, multi-tenant AI knowledge platform with chat, voice, and WhatsApp capabilities. The page follows a demo-led approach with an embedded live `<raven-chat>` widget in the hero section.
12	
13	## Target Audience
14	
15	- **Primary**: Developers and technical leads evaluating Raven for their team
16	- **Secondary**: Business decision makers (CTO/VP Eng) looking for enterprise features and compliance
17	- **Approach**: Developer-first copy with enterprise trust signals layered in
18	
19	## Design System
20	
21	### Color Palette (Tailwind)
22	
23	| Token | Dark Theme | Light Theme | Usage |
24	|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
25	| Background primary | `#000000` / `black` | `#ffffff` / `white` | Page background |
26	| Background secondary | `#0a0a0a` / `neutral-950` | `#fafafa` / `neutral-50` | Card/section backgrounds |
27	| Background tertiary | `#1a1a1a` / `neutral-900` | `#f5f5f5` / `neutral-100` | Inset panels, code blocks |
28	| Border | `#262626` / `neutral-800` | `#e5e5e5` / `neutral-200` | Card borders, dividers |
29	| Border hover | `#404040` / `neutral-700` | `#d4d4d4` / `neutral-300` | Interactive borders |
30	| Text primary | `#ffffff` / `white` | `#000000` / `black` | Headlines, body |
31	| Text secondary | `#a3a3a3` / `neutral-400` | `#525252` / `neutral-600` | Descriptions, subtext |
32	| Text muted | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | Captions, labels |
33	| Accent | `#fbbf24` / `amber-400` | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | CTAs, active indicators, highlights |
34	| Accent hover | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | `#d97706` / `amber-600` | Button hover states |
35	
36	### Theme Behavior
37	
38	- Dark/light toggle in nav (sun/moon icon)
39	- Default: respects `prefers-color-scheme` system preference
40	- Implemented via Tailwind `dark:` class variant on `<html>` element
41	- Raven logo: black bird on light theme, white bird on dark theme
42	
43	### Typography
44	
45	- **Headlines**: Bold/extrabold, larger sizes
46	- **Section headers**: Bold, provocative tone (problem-first hooks)
47	- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible language
48	- **Technical details**: Muted text, smaller size, surfaces in feature deep-dives
49	
50	### Content Tone
51	
52	- **Section headers**: Bold and opinionated — *"Your team's knowledge is trapped. Free it."*
53	- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible — *"Ingest any document, ask in natural language, get cited answers."*
54	- **Feature deep-dives**: Technical specifics — *"Hybrid search combining pgvector cosine similarity with BM25 full-text ranking"*
55	- **GitHub/README**: Engineering-precise tone (separate from landing page)
56	
57	## Page Structure
58	
59	### Section 1: Navigation Bar
60	
61	**Layout**: Sticky top nav, full-width, blurred background
62	
63	**Elements**:
64	- Left: Raven logo (themed — black on light, white on dark)
65	- Center: Features, Pricing, Docs, GitHub (with stars badge)
66	- Right: Theme toggle (sun/moon), Sign In (ghost button), Get Started Free (amber accent button)
67	
68	**Mobile**: Hamburger menu, Get Started Free always visible
69	
70	### Section 2: Hero (Centered, Demo-Led)
71	
72	**Layout**: Centered text block → trust badges → live chat widget
73	
74	**Content**:
75	- Badge: `Open Source · MIT Licensed` (small uppercase, amber text)
76	- Headline: **"The AI Brain for Your Entire Team"**
77	- Subhead: "Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. Self-host or use our cloud."
78	- CTAs: `Get Started Free` (amber filled) + `Try Live Demo ↓` (ghost/outline)
79	- Trust bar: 🔓 MIT Licensed · 🐳 4 Deploy Options · 🔑 Bring Your Own LLM Keys · 🛡️ Full Data Sovereignty
80	
81	**Live Demo Widget**:
82	- Embedded `<raven-chat>` web component
83	- Connected to a demo knowledge base (Raven's own docs)
84	- Header: "💬 Try Raven Live — Ask anything" + "Demo Knowledge Base" badge
85	- Pre-populated with a sample Q&A showing cited answer
86	- Input field with Send button (amber accent)
87	
88	### Section 3: Problem Statement
89	
90	**Headline**: "Your team's knowledge is scattered. Your people are stuck."
91	
92	**Layout**: 3 cards in a row (stacks on mobile)
93	
94	| Card | Headline | Copy |
95	|------|----------|------|
96	| 1 | Docs nobody reads | Knowledge lives in PDFs, wikis, and Confluence pages that go stale. Your team asks the same questions on Slack instead. |
97	| 2 | Search that doesn't work | Keyword search returns 50 results. None of them answer the actual question. Your team gives up and pings someone senior. |
98	| 3 | No way to just ask | Your team wants to ask a question and get an answer — with a source. Not browse. Not search. Just ask. |
99	
100	**Transition line**: "Raven fixes this. Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp."
101	
102	### Section 4: Tabbed Features
103	
104	**Layout**: 3 tabs with active indicator (amber underline). Each tab shows screenshot on one side + description on the other. Alternating layout (screenshot left/right) for visual rhythm.
105	
106	**Tab 1: AI Chat**
107	- Headline: "Ask your docs anything"
108	- Copy: Semantic search powered by hybrid vector + BM25 ranking. Every answer comes with source citations. Embed the chatbot widget on your site with a single `<raven-chat>` tag.
109	- Visual: Screenshot of chat interface with cited answer
110	
111	**Tab 2: Voice Agent**
112	- Headline: "Talk to your knowledge base"
113	- Copy: Real-time voice calls via WebRTC. Hands-free access to your entire knowledge base — powered by LiveKit with STT, LLM, and TTS pipeline. Perfect for field teams and mobile workers.
114	- Visual: Screenshot of voice call UI
115	
116	**Tab 3: WhatsApp**
117	- Headline: "Meet your team where they already are"
118	- Copy: Connect via WhatsApp Business API. Zero app installs, zero training. Your team asks questions in the chat they already use every day.
119	- Visual: Screenshot of WhatsApp conversation with Raven
120	
121	### Section 5: How It Works
122	
123	**Layout**: 3 numbered cards in horizontal row, connected by a line/arrow
124	
125	| Step | Headline | Copy |
126	|------|----------|------|
127	| 1 | **Ingest** | Upload PDFs, DOCX, Markdown, HTML, or paste URLs. Raven chunks, embeds, and indexes everything automatically. |
128	| 2 | **Search** | Hybrid retrieval — pgvector cosine similarity + BM25 full-text ranking fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. No keyword guessing. |
129	| 3 | **Answer** | Your team asks in natural language. Raven responds with accurate, cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. |
130	
131	Technical details in muted text below each card for developer audience.
132	
133	### Section 6: Deploy Your Way
134	
135	**Headline**: "Your infrastructure. Your rules."
136

file.content:
# Raven Landing Page — Design Spec

**Date**: 2026-04-13
**Domain**: raven.ravencloak.org
**Status**: Approved

---

## Overview

A full-platform marketing landing page for Raven — an open-source, self-hostable, multi-tenant AI knowledge platform with chat, voice, and WhatsApp capabilities. The page follows a demo-led approach with an embedded live `<raven-chat>` widget in the hero section.

## Target Audience

- **Primary**: Developers and technical leads evaluating Raven for their team
- **Secondary**: Business decision makers (CTO/VP Eng) looking for enterprise features and compliance
- **Approach**: Developer-first copy with enterprise trust signals layered in

## Design System

### Color Palette (Tailwind)

| Token | Dark Theme | Light Theme | Usage |
|-------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| Background primary | `#000000` / `black` | `#ffffff` / `white` | Page background |
| Background secondary | `#0a0a0a` / `neutral-950` | `#fafafa` / `neutral-50` | Card/section backgrounds |
| Background tertiary | `#1a1a1a` / `neutral-900` | `#f5f5f5` / `neutral-100` | Inset panels, code blocks |
| Border | `#262626` / `neutral-800` | `#e5e5e5` / `neutral-200` | Card borders, dividers |
| Border hover | `#404040` / `neutral-700` | `#d4d4d4` / `neutral-300` | Interactive borders |
| Text primary | `#ffffff` / `white` | `#000000` / `black` | Headlines, body |
| Text secondary | `#a3a3a3` / `neutral-400` | `#525252` / `neutral-600` | Descriptions, subtext |
| Text muted | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | `#737373` / `neutral-500` | Captions, labels |
| Accent | `#fbbf24` / `amber-400` | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | CTAs, active indicators, highlights |
| Accent hover | `#f59e0b` / `amber-500` | `#d97706` / `amber-600` | Button hover states |

### Theme Behavior

- Dark/light toggle in nav (sun/moon icon)
- Default: respects `prefers-color-scheme` system preference
- Implemented via Tailwind `dark:` class variant on `<html>` element
- Raven logo: black bird on light theme, white bird on dark theme

### Typography

- **Headlines**: Bold/extrabold, larger sizes
- **Section headers**: Bold, provocative tone (problem-first hooks)
- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible language
- **Technical details**: Muted text, smaller size, surfaces in feature deep-dives

### Content Tone

- **Section headers**: Bold and opinionated — *"Your team's knowledge is trapped. Free it."*
- **Body copy**: Clear and accessible — *"Ingest any document, ask in natural language, get cited answers."*
- **Feature deep-dives**: Technical specifics — *"Hybrid search combining pgvector cosine similarity with BM25 full-text ranking"*
- **GitHub/README**: Engineering-precise tone (separate from landing page)

## Page Structure

### Section 1: Navigation Bar

**Layout**: Sticky top nav, full-width, blurred background

**Elements**:
- Left: Raven logo (themed — black on light, white on dark)
- Center: Features, Pricing, Docs, GitHub (with stars badge)
- Right: Theme toggle (sun/moon), Sign In (ghost button), Get Started Free (amber accent button)

**Mobile**: Hamburger menu, Get Started Free always visible

### Section 2: Hero (Centered, Demo-Led)

**Layout**: Centered text block → trust badges → live chat widget

**Content**:
- Badge: `Open Source · MIT Licensed` (small uppercase, amber text)
- Headline: **"The AI Brain for Your Entire Team"**
- Subhead: "Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. Self-host or use our cloud."
- CTAs: `Get Started Free` (amber filled) + `Try Live Demo ↓` (ghost/outline)
- Trust bar: 🔓 MIT Licensed · 🐳 4 Deploy Options · 🔑 Bring Your Own LLM Keys · 🛡️ Full Data Sovereignty

**Live Demo Widget**:
- Embedded `<raven-chat>` web component
- Connected to a demo knowledge base (Raven's own docs)
- Header: "💬 Try Raven Live — Ask anything" + "Demo Knowledge Base" badge
- Pre-populated with a sample Q&A showing cited answer
- Input field with Send button (amber accent)

### Section 3: Problem Statement

**Headline**: "Your team's knowledge is scattered. Your people are stuck."

**Layout**: 3 cards in a row (stacks on mobile)

| Card | Headline | Copy |
|------|----------|------|
| 1 | Docs nobody reads | Knowledge lives in PDFs, wikis, and Confluence pages that go stale. Your team asks the same questions on Slack instead. |
| 2 | Search that doesn't work | Keyword search returns 50 results. None of them answer the actual question. Your team gives up and pings someone senior. |
| 3 | No way to just ask | Your team wants to ask a question and get an answer — with a source. Not browse. Not search. Just ask. |

**Transition line**: "Raven fixes this. Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp."

### Section 4: Tabbed Features

**Layout**: 3 tabs with active indicator (amber underline). Each tab shows screenshot on one side + description on the other. Alternating layout (screenshot left/right) for visual rhythm.

**Tab 1: AI Chat**
- Headline: "Ask your docs anything"
- Copy: Semantic search powered by hybrid vector + BM25 ranking. Every answer comes with source citations. Embed the chatbot widget on your site with a single `<raven-chat>` tag.
- Visual: Screenshot of chat interface with cited answer

**Tab 2: Voice Agent**
- Headline: "Talk to your knowledge base"
- Copy: Real-time voice calls via WebRTC. Hands-free access to your entire knowledge base — powered by LiveKit with STT, LLM, and TTS pipeline. Perfect for field teams and mobile workers.
- Visual: Screenshot of voice call UI

**Tab 3: WhatsApp**
- Headline: "Meet your team where they already are"
- Copy: Connect via WhatsApp Business API. Zero app installs, zero training. Your team asks questions in the chat they already use every day.
- Visual: Screenshot of WhatsApp conversation with Raven

### Section 5: How It Works

**Layout**: 3 numbered cards in horizontal row, connected by a line/arrow

| Step | Headline | Copy |
|------|----------|------|
| 1 | **Ingest** | Upload PDFs, DOCX, Markdown, HTML, or paste URLs. Raven chunks, embeds, and indexes everything automatically. |
| 2 | **Search** | Hybrid retrieval — pgvector cosine similarity + BM25 full-text ranking fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. No keyword guessing. |
| 3 | **Answer** | Your team asks in natural language. Raven responds with accurate, cited answers — via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. |

Technical details in muted text below each card for developer audience.

### Section 6: Deploy Your Way

**Headline**: "Your infrastructure. Your rules."

**Layout**: 4 cards in a grid (2x2 on desktop, stacks on mobile)

| Option | Icon | Copy |
|--------|------|------|
| Docker Compose | 🐳 | One command. Full stack. Perfect for getting started and small teams. |
| Kubernetes | ☸️ | Production-grade, multi-region, auto-scaling. Helm charts included. |
| EC2 + Ansible | 🖥️ | Cloud VM with auto-TLS via Traefik. Playbooks ready to go. |
| Edge / Raspberry Pi | 📡 | 25MB ARM64 binary. Run the API on a Pi, connect a remote AI worker via gRPC. |

### Section 7: Enterprise Ready

**Headline**: "Built for teams that don't compromise."

**Layout**: 6 cards in a 3x2 grid (stacks on mobile)

| Card | Copy |
|------|------|
| Multi-Tenant Isolation | Full Row-Level Security per tenant in PostgreSQL. Your data never crosses boundaries. |
| SSO / SCIM | Keycloak-powered OIDC, SAML, and SCIM provisioning out of the box. |
| Audit Logs | Immutable, timestamped audit trail for every action. Compliance-ready. |
| BYOK | Bring your own LLM keys — Anthropic, OpenAI, Cohere, or self-hosted models. Zero vendor lock-in. |
| eBPF WAF | Kernel-level XDP pre-filtering and observability at the edge. |
| GDPR / SOC2 | Built for compliance. Self-host for full data sovereignty. |

### Section 8: Pricing

**Headline**: "Simple pricing. Generous free tier."

**Layout**: 3 tier cards side-by-side. Pro card has amber "Recommended" badge and slightly elevated/bordered treatment.

| | Free | Pro (Recommended) | Enterprise |
|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $0 forever | $29/month | Contact Sales |
| Users | 5 | 25 | Unlimited |
| Workspaces | 2 | 10 | Unlimited |
| Knowledge Bases | 3 | 50 | Unlimited |
| Storage | 500 MB | 10 GB | Unlimited |
| Voice Sessions | 1 concurrent | 5 concurrent | Unlimited |
| Voice Minutes | 60/mo | 1,200/mo | Unlimited |
| Key Features | Full MIT source, AI chat/voice/WhatsApp, BYOK, self-host | Everything in Free + higher limits, advanced rate limiting | Everything in Pro + SAML/SSO/SCIM, audit logs, eBPF WAF, dedicated support, custom SLA |
| CTA | Get Started Free | Start Pro Trial | Contact Sales |

### Section 9: Bottom CTA

**Headline**: "Start building with Raven in under 5 minutes."

**Code snippet**: `docker compose up -d`

**CTA**: `Get Started Free` (amber accent, large)

### Section 10: Footer

**Layout**: 4-column grid (stacks on mobile)

| Product | Resources | Community | Legal |
|---------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Features | Quick Start | GitHub | Privacy Policy |
| Pricing | API Reference | Discord | Terms of Service |
| Docs | Architecture | Contributing | MIT License |
| Changelog | Blog | Roadmap | |

**Bottom row**: © 2026 Ravencloak · Built with ❤️ for teams that own their data

## Technical Implementation

### Stack
- **Framework**: Static HTML + Tailwind CSS (paid license — Tailwind Plus/UI components)
- **Theme**: Tailwind `dark:` class variant, toggled via JavaScript on `<html>` element
- **Chat Widget**: Embedded `<raven-chat>` web component pointing to demo API
- **Hosting**: Static site at raven.ravencloak.org (Cloudflare Pages or similar CDN)
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first, breakpoints at `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`

### Assets Required
- Raven logo (SVG, themed — black and white variants)
- Product screenshots for Chat, Voice, WhatsApp features (from existing admin dashboard)
- Icons for deployment options and enterprise features (Tailwind Heroicons or similar)

### Demo Knowledge Base
- A curated knowledge base containing Raven's own documentation
- Hosted on the production API or a dedicated demo instance
- The `<raven-chat>` widget connects via a domain-scoped API key
- Rate-limited for public access (anonymous users, read-only)

### Performance Targets
- First Contentful Paint < 1.5s
- Total page weight < 500KB (excluding chat widget)
- Lighthouse score > 90 across all categories

## Open Questions

1. **Raven logo**: Does a finalized SVG logo exist, or do we need to create one?
2. **Demo instance**: Is there a running demo API we can point the chat widget to, or do we need to set one up?
3. **Product screenshots**: Can we capture these from the existing admin dashboard, or do we need to mock them?
4. **Domain setup**: Is raven.ravencloak.org DNS already configured?

## Out of Scope

- User authentication / sign-up flow (handled by app.ravencloak.org)
- Blog / changelog pages (future addition)
- Documentation site (separate docs.ravencloak.org)
- A/B testing / analytics integration (PostHog — future addition)

input.new_string:
### Stack
- **Framework**: Static HTML + Tailwind CSS v4 (paid license — Tailwind Plus/UI components)
- **Build**: Tailwind CLI with purging for production (NOT the CDN play script — must meet <500KB target)
- **Theme**: Tailwind `dark:` class variant, toggled via JavaScript on `<html>` element, default from `prefers-color-scheme`
- **Chat Widget**: Embedded `<raven-chat>` web component (mock mode at launch, live API post-launch)
- **Hosting**: Static site at raven.ravencloak.org (Cloudflare Pages)
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first, breakpoints at `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`
- **Font loading**: Inter via Google Fonts with `font-display: swap`

### SEO & Meta Tags

```html
<title>Raven — The AI Brain for Your Entire Team</title>
<meta name="description" content="Open-source AI knowledge platform. Ingest your docs, ask in natural language, get cited answers via chat, voice, or WhatsApp. Self-host or use our cloud.">
<meta property="og:title" content="Raven — Open Source AI Knowledge Platform">
<meta property="og:description" content="Ingest your docs. Ask in natural language. Get cited answers via chat, voice, or WhatsApp.">
<meta property="og:url" content="https://raven.ravencloak.org">
<meta property="og:image" content="https://raven.ravencloak.org/og-image.png">
<meta property="og:type" content="website">
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
<link rel="canonical" href="https://raven.ravencloak.org">
```

OG image: 1200x630px, Raven logo + tagline on dark background. Created as a static asset.

### Domain Map

| Purpose | Domain |
|---------|--------|
| Marketing/landing page | `raven.ravencloak.org` |
| Application (dashboard) | `app.ravencloak.org` |
| API server | `api.ravencloak.org` |
| Auth (Keycloak) | `auth.ravencloak.org` |
| Documentation | `docs.ravencloak.org` (future) |

### Accessibility

- Amber accent (`#fbbf24`) on black background: contrast ratio ~11.6:1 (passes WCAG AA and AAA)
- Muted text (`#737373`) on black: contrast ratio ~4.6:1 (passes AA for normal text)
- All interactive elements: visible focus ring (`ring-2 ring-amber-400 ring-offset-2 ring-offset-black`)
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: disable scroll animations
- Semantic HTML: `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<footer>` with `aria-label` on each section

### Interactions & Animations

- **Scroll animations**: Subtle fade-in-up on section entry (via `IntersectionObserver`), respects `prefers-reduced-motion`
- **Tab switching** (Section 4): Click to switch, amber underline slides to active tab, screenshot crossfades. On mobile: tabs become a vertical accordion
- **Hover states**: Cards lift with `hover:-translate-y-1 hover:border-amber-400/50` transition
- **Theme toggle**: Smooth color transition via `transition-colors duration-300` on `<html>`
- **GitHub stars**: Static count at build time (hardcoded, updated periodically) — avoids GitHub API rate limits

1. **Enterprise pricing** — added note that backend has $99/mo but landing page intentionally shows "Contact Sales"

3. **Chat widget mock** — changed to Phase 1 (mock mode) / Phase 2 (live API) approach

6. **SEO/meta tags** — added full section with OG tags, canonical URL, domain map
7. **Typography** — added concrete Tailwind classes and Inter font spec
